back_translation: Jehoshaphat rested with his fathers and was buried with them in the City of David, and Jehoram his son reigned in his place.
Jehoram had brothers, the sons of Jehoshaphat: Azariah, Jehiel, Zechariah, Azariahu, Michael, and Shephatiah; all these were sons of Jehoshaphat king of Israel.
Their father had given them many gifts of silver, gold, and precious things, along with fortified cities in Judah, but he gave the kingdom to Jehoram, because he was the firstborn.
When Jehoram had ascended over his father's kingdom and established himself, he killed all his brothers with the sword, along with some of the princes of Israel.
Jehoram was thirty-two years old when he became king, and he reigned eight years in Jerusalem.
He walked in the way of the kings of Israel, as the house of Ahab had done, for he had a daughter of Ahab as his wife; and he did evil in the eyes of the LORD.
Yet the LORD was unwilling to destroy the house of David because of the covenant He had made with David, and because He had promised to give a lamp to David and his descendants forever.
In his days Edom rebelled against the authority of Judah and set up a king over themselves.
So Jehoram crossed over with his commanders and all his chariots; when the Edomites surrounded him and the chariot commanders, he rose up by night and broke through.
So Edom rebelled against the authority of Judah to this day. At the same time Libnah also rebelled against his authority, because he had forsaken the LORD, the God of his fathers.
He also built high places on the hills of Judah, and led the inhabitants of Jerusalem to be unfaithful to God, and made Judah go astray.
A letter came to him from Elijah the prophet, saying, “Thus says the LORD, the God of David your father: ‘Because you have not walked in the ways of Jehoshaphat your father or of Asa king of Judah,

but have walked in the way of the kings of Israel and have led Judah and the inhabitants of Jerusalem to be unfaithful, as the house of Ahab did, and also have killed your brothers, your father's house, who were better than you,
behold, the LORD will strike your people, your sons, your wives, and all your possessions with a severe plague.
And you yourself will suffer a severe illness, a disease of your bowels, until your bowels come out because of the disease, day after day.’”
Then the LORD stirred up against Jehoram the spirit of the Philistines and the Arabs who lived near the Cushites.
They came up against Judah, invaded it, and carried off all the possessions found in the royal palace, along with his sons and wives, so that no son was left to him except Jehoahaz, his youngest son.
After all this, the LORD afflicted him with an incurable disease of the bowels.
In the course of time, at the end of two years, his bowels came out because of the disease, and he died in severe pain. His people made no fire in his honor, as they had for his fathers.

He was thirty-two years old when he became king, and he reigned eight years in Jerusalem. He departed with no one's regret, and they buried him in the City of David, but not in the tombs of the kings.
